title: SourceCredentialBinding
description: The persisted form of a source credential (design §13): an operator-managed REFERENCE to secret material held in an external secret store, plus non-sensitive auth metadata. This document NEVER contains secret material — only a SecretRef (scheme://locator[#version]) the runner dereferences at transport-bind time via ISecretResolver. Backends store this document verbatim.

sourceName.description: The Arazzo sourceDescriptions name this credential authenticates calls to.

environment.description: The deployment environment the binding applies to (e.g. production, staging); the runner caches by (sourceName, environment).

authKind.description: The HTTP auth scheme the resolved secret(s) build into an IHttpAuthenticationProvider. Non-sensitive metadata so the runner knows how to apply the credential.

secretRefs.description: The named references to secret material in the external secret store (at least one). Each is a pointer, never the secret itself: the runner's ISecretResolver dereferences them. For apiKey/bearer one ref named 'value'; for basic a 'password'; for oauth2ClientCredentials a 'clientSecret'.

config.description: Non-secret auth configuration as KVP entries (e.g. the apiKey header name, the OAuth token endpoint, scopes, a basic-auth username). Secret material must never appear here — it lives behind a secretRef.

createdBy.description: The actor that created the binding.

lastUpdatedBy.description: The actor that last updated the binding, if any.

lastUpdatedAt.description: When the binding was last updated, if ever.

SecretReference.title: SecretReference
SecretReference.description: A named, role-tagged reference to one secret in the external store. The 'ref' is a SecretRef of the form scheme://locator[#version] (keyvault, awssm, vault, env, file); rotation is by changing the ref (typically its #version), so the control plane never reads the secret.

name.description: The role this secret plays in building the provider (e.g. value, password, clientSecret).

CredentialConfigEntry.title: CredentialConfigEntry
CredentialConfigEntry.description: A single non-secret auth configuration value.

key.description: The configuration key (e.g. headerName, tokenUrl, scopes, username).
